model:
(define-fun __need_malloc_and_calloc () Bool
  false)
(define-fun __STRICT_ANSI__ () Bool
  true)
(define-fun __USE_EXTERN_INLINES () Bool
  false)
(define-fun _STDLIB_H () Bool
  false)
(define-fun BB_FEATURE_LS_USERNAME () Bool
  true)
(define-fun BB_FEATURE_LS_FILETYPES () Bool
  true)
(define-fun __need___FILE () Bool
  false)
reasoning: info is uninitialized at printf at L16 when FILETYPES=T AND !USERNAME — known bug (// ERROR at L16). The PC includes both BB_FEATURE_LS_USERNAME=T (where info IS initialized at L11, no bug) and !USERNAME (bug exists), making it under-constrained in that dimension. However, the PC also requires restrictive library macros (!_STDLIB_H, __STRICT_ANSI__) which are not satisfied in normal compilation. The restrictive library macros prevent the alarm from triggering in normal FILETYPES=T AND !USERNAME configurations. Classified as TRUE_OVER: the PC is over-constrained due to restrictive library macros.
